short_desc: Print the current working directory
scenario: You've lost track of where you are in the file system and need to confirm your current location

challenges.prompt: Print your current location in the file system

short_desc: List the contents of a directory
scenario: You want to see all the files and folders in your current directory

common_flags.desc: Show detailed list with permissions and sizes
Show hidden files
Show file sizes in human readable format

challenges.prompt: List all files in the current directory
List all files including hidden ones with full details and human readable sizes

short_desc: Change the current directory
scenario: You want to navigate into a folder called projects

challenges.prompt: Navigate to your home directory
Navigate to the previous directory you were in

short_desc: Copy a file or directory

common_flags.desc: Copy directories recursively
Prompt before overwriting

challenges.prompt: Copy a file called notes.txt to a folder called backup
Copy a folder called projects and all its contents to a folder called projects_backup

short_desc: Move or rename a file or directory
scenario: You want to rename a file called old-name.txt to new-name.txt

common_flags.desc: Prompt before overwriting

challenges.prompt: Rename a file called draft.txt to final.txt
Move a folder called reports into a folder called archive without being prompted if something already exists there

short_desc: Remove a file or directory
scenario: You want to delete a temporary file you no longer need

common_flags.desc: Remove directories recursively
Force removal without prompting

challenges.prompt: Delete a file called temp.txt
Delete a directory called tmp and everything inside it without being prompted

short_desc: Create a new directory
scenario: You want to create a new folder called reports in your current location

common_flags.desc: Create parent directories as needed

challenges.prompt: Create a new folder called documents
Create a nested directory structure of projects/2024/reports in one command

short_desc: Create an empty file or update a file's timestamp
scenario: You want to quickly create a new empty file called notes.txt

challenges.prompt: Create a new empty file called notes.txt
Create three empty files called a.txt b.txt and c.txt in one command

short_desc: Search for files and directories
scenario: You want to locate all .log files anywhere under your current directory

common_flags.desc: Search by filename or pattern
Filter by type, f for file, d for directory

challenges.prompt: Find all files with the .txt extension in the current directory
Find all .log files anywhere under the current directory and show only files not directories

short_desc: Quickly find files by name using a pre-built index
scenario: You want to find a file called nginx.conf anywhere on the system without waiting for a full search

common_flags.desc: Case-insensitive search

challenges.prompt: Search for a file called nginx.conf on the system
Search for a file called nginx.conf without caring about upper or lower case

short_desc: Print the contents of a file to the terminal
scenario: You want to quickly read the contents of a small config file

common_flags.desc: Show line numbers

challenges.prompt: Print the contents of a file called config.yml to the terminal
Print the contents of config.yml with line numbers

short_desc: View file contents one page at a time
scenario: You want to read a large log file without flooding your terminal

common_flags.desc: Show line numbers

challenges.prompt: Open a large file called server.log for comfortable reading
Open server.log for reading with line numbers visible

short_desc: Show the first lines of a file
scenario: You want to quickly check the top 10 lines of a log file

common_flags.desc: Specify how many lines to show

challenges.prompt: Show the first 10 lines of a file called server.log
Show the first 25 lines of a file called server.log

short_desc: Show the last lines of a file
scenario: You want to watch a log file update in real time as events come in

common_flags.desc: Specify how many lines to show
Follow the file and show new lines as they are added

challenges.prompt: Show the last 10 lines of a file called server.log

short_desc: Open a simple terminal text editor
scenario: You need to make a quick edit to a config file directly in the terminal

challenges.prompt: Open a file called config.yml in a simple terminal text editor

short_desc: Open a powerful but more complex terminal text editor
scenario: You need to edit a file on a remote server where no other editor is available

challenges.prompt: Open a file called config.yml in vim
Open a file called config.yml in vim and jump straight to line 42

short_desc: Compare two files line by line and show differences
scenario: You want to see what changed between your original config and an updated version

common_flags.desc: Show differences in unified format, easier to read

challenges.prompt: Compare two files called original.yml and updated.yml
Compare original.yml and updated.yml in a format that clearly shows added and removed lines

short_desc: Change the permissions of a file or directory
scenario: You want to make a shell script executable so you can run it

common_flags.desc: Apply changes recursively to a directory

challenges.prompt: Make a file called deploy.sh executable
Make a file called report.txt read-only for everyone

short_desc: Change the owner of a file or directory

common_flags.desc: Apply changes recursively to a directory

challenges.prompt: Change the owner of a file called app.conf to a user called deploy
Recursively change the owner and group of a folder called /var/www to a user called www-data

short_desc: Run a command with superuser privileges
scenario: You need to install a package but get a permissions error as a regular user

challenges.prompt: Install a package called nginx using apt as a superuser

common_flags.desc: Switch and load the target user's full environment

challenges.prompt: Switch to a user called deploy

short_desc: Search for a pattern in text or files
scenario: You want to find every line containing the word error in a log file

Case-insensitive search
Show line numbers alongside matches

challenges.prompt: Search for the word error in a file called server.log

short_desc: Process and extract columns from structured text

challenges.prompt: Print the first column of a file called data.txt

short_desc: Find and replace text in a file or stream

short_desc: Sort lines of text alphabetically or numerically
scenario: You have a list of names in a file and want them sorted alphabetically

common_flags.desc: Sort in reverse order

challenges.prompt: Sort the lines of a file called names.txt alphabetically
Sort the lines of a file called scores.txt numerically in reverse order

short_desc: Remove duplicate consecutive lines from output

short_desc: Count lines, words, or characters in a file
scenario: You want to know how many lines are in a log file

common_flags.desc: Count lines only
Count words only
Count characters only

challenges.prompt: Count the number of lines in a file called server.log
Count the total number of words across all .txt files in the current directory

short_desc: Extract specific columns or fields from text

challenges.prompt: Extract the first field from a colon separated file called data.txt

short_desc: Translate or delete characters in text

short_desc: Show a live updating view of running processes and resource usage
scenario: Your system feels slow and you want to see what is consuming the most CPU in real time

challenges.prompt: Open an interactive live view of system processes and resource usage

short_desc: An improved interactive process viewer with a cleaner interface than top
scenario: You want a more readable and navigable version of top with colour coding

challenges.prompt: Open an improved interactive process viewer

short_desc: Terminate a process by its process ID

challenges.prompt: Terminate a process with the ID 3847

short_desc: List processes currently running in the background of your terminal session
scenario: You have sent a few processes to the background and want to see what is running

challenges.prompt: List all processes currently running in the background of your terminal session

challenges.prompt: Resume the most recently suspended process in the background
Resume the second suspended job in the background

short_desc: Bring a background process back to the foreground
scenario: You have a process running in the background and want to interact with it again

challenges.prompt: Bring the most recent background process back to the foreground

short_desc: Show available and used disk space on mounted filesystems

short_desc: Show how much disk space a file or directory is using

Show sizes in human readable format

challenges.prompt: Check the total size of a folder called projects

short_desc: Show total, used and available memory on the system
scenario: You want to check how much RAM is currently being used on a server

challenges.prompt: Check how much RAM is currently available on the system

short_desc: Print system information including kernel and architecture
scenario: You want to confirm what operating system and kernel version a server is running

challenges.prompt: Check what operating system the current machine is running

short_desc: Show how long the system has been running and current load
scenario: You want to check whether a server was recently rebooted and what the current load average is

challenges.prompt: Check how long the system has been running

short_desc: Print the username of the currently logged in user
scenario: You are on an unfamiliar server and want to confirm which user account you are logged in as

challenges.prompt: Check which user account you are currently logged in as

challenges.prompt: Check if google.com is reachable from your machine

Send data in the request body
Write output to a file instead of the terminal

short_desc: Download a file from a URL

short_desc: Show or manage network interfaces and routing
